rulings.Case Title: GARY E GROTE et al VS. DAVID DRIVER et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 18, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANTS DAVID DRIVER, EMILIA RALLAPALLI, KRISHNA RALLAPALLI, AND PHILIPPINE RALLAPALLI'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PURSUANT TO CRC 2.112 PLAINTIFFS MUST PLEAD WHICH PLAINTIFF IS ASSERTING A CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST WHICH DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFFS MUST ALLEGE FACTS THAT SUPPORT CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST IDENTIFIED DEFENDANTS. PLAINTIFFS MUST ALLEGE CONDUCT THAT WAS WRONGFUL BY SOME LEGAL MEASURE OTHER THAN THE FACT OF THE INTERFERENCE ITSELF TO SUPPORT CLAIMS FOR INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE. =(302/CWW/AM-X)

rulings.Case Title: GARY E GROTE et al VS. DAVID DRIVER et al

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCANS FOR LIVABLE NEIGHBORHOODS, AN UNIN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Mtn For Reconsideration Of Order Concerning Writ And Related Motions Or For Supplemental Equitable Relief
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 18, 2009, line 6, PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCANS FOR LIVABLE NEIGHBORHOODS Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Concerning Writ And Related Motions Or For Supplemental Equitable Relief. THE COURT GRANTS RECONSIDERATION OF ITS PRIOR ORDER AS TO THESE FIVE ITEMS. THE FOCUS OF THE PRIOR HEARING WAS WHETHER THE COURT SHOULD ISSUE A GENERIC WRIT OR WHETHER IT WOULD MERELY RESTRAIN ENFORCEMENT OF THE POLICIES SPECIFICALLY CITED IN THE COURT OF APPEAL OPINION. THE COURT RETAINED JURISDICTION OVER THIS MATTER TO ENSURE CEQA COMPLIANCE AND THESE FIVE ITEMS HAVE NOW BEEN FULLY BRIEFED. WRIT AMENDED, RESTRAINING CCSF FROM ENFORCING MODIFIED OBJECTIVE 11, 2004 MODIFIED OBJECTIVE 1 AS INTERPRETED BY ITS EXPLANATORY TEXT, NEW LANGUAGE ADDED TO MODIFIED IMPLEMENTATION 1.3, AND NEW LANGUAGE ADDED TO MODIFIED POLICY 4.4. MOTION DENIED AS TO 2004 MODIFIED POLICY 4.5. =(302/CWW/PB)
